name: GPT-5.4
archDisplay: Undisclosed architecture (presumed dense transformer, reasoning-tuned); first general-purpose OpenAI model with native computer-use; ships in Thinking/Pro/mini/nano tiers
note: Flagship/'Thinking' tier of the GPT-5.4 family, confirmed real via TechCrunch and The Decoder launch coverage. GPT-5.4 Pro ($30/$180 per M, ARC-AGI-2 83.3%, GDPval 82%) and cheaper GPT-5.4 mini/nano also shipped the same month but are out of scope for this pass (not requested).

results.notes: Humanity's Last Exam, no tools (with-tools variant scores 52.1; Pro no-tools scores 42.7)
ARC-AGI-2; Pro variant scores 83.3 (cross-confirmed by deeplearning.ai's The Batch, which reported GPT-5.2 Pro at 54.2% vs GPT-5.4 Pro at 83.3%)
SWE-Bench Pro (harder variant of SWE-bench; not the base 'swe-bench' slug)

OSWorld-Verified score for GPT-5.4 standard/Thinking tier, found in the same the-decoder.com launch-day comparison table already used as the source for this model's other 9 results (directly re-fetched and reconfirmed present in this pass, in the row 'OSWorld-Verified | GPT-5.4 75.0% | GPT-5.4 Pro - | GPT-5.3 Codex 74.0% | GPT-5.2 47.3% | GPT-5.2 Pro -'). GPT-5.4 Pro has no published number for this benchmark in the same table.

Agents' Last Exam (ALE-CLI, Overall leaderboard), OpenClaw harness, High reasoning effort: Pass rate 20.5% / Score 37.3% (rank 22 overall). Score component used per corpus convention. Mirrored from agents-last-exam.org/leaderboard.

SWE-bench Verified (the base 'swe-bench' slug -- distinct from the harder SWE-Bench Pro figure already tracked here under swe-bench-pro) from Epoch AI's independent evaluation of GPT-5.4, relayed via themodelbeat.com.
Consistent with this corpus's existing GPT-5.x AIME 2025 trajectory (94.6/100/94/100 for GPT-5/GPT-5-Pro/GPT-5.1/GPT-5.2).

Value extracted from the page's CSS bar-fill (--target-width:84.141%); extraction method cross-validated against the same page's GPQA Diamond figure, which closely matches this file's already-confirmed GPQA numbers.

Value extracted from the page's CSS bar-fill (--target-width:87.514%); extraction method cross-validated against the same page's GPQA Diamond figure, which closely matches this file's already-confirmed GPQA numbers.

verificationNotes:
Existence and March 5, 2026 launch date confirmed via TechCrunch ('OpenAI launches GPT-5.4 with Pro and Thinking versions') and Wikipedia. Pricing ($2.50/$15 per M) and 1M-token context (922K in / 128K out) cross-confirmed by The Decoder and LayerLens independently. Nine benchmark scores below (GDPval, FrontierMath T1-3/T4, GPQA Diamond, HLE, ARC-AGI-1/2, SWE-Bench Pro, BrowseComp) taken from The Decoder's launch-day writeup, which itself relays OpenAI's own announced figures (treated as self-reported per instructions). Could NOT confirm a separately-branded, separately-benchmarked 'GPT-5.4 (High)' tier distinct from the flagship numbers below or from the 'xhigh' config tracked by Artificial Analysis (AA composite Intelligence Index score 51 at launch, later cited as 57 in a follow-up AA post reflecting an index-methodology revision) — so no High-tier sibling was added; see 'uncertain'.

[2026-07-16] Resolved the 6 missing-core-benchmark gaps and the swe-bench=76.9 comparability flag. AIME: added 95.3% (AIME 2024/2025 combined) from Epoch AI's independent evaluation, found in the same themodelbeat.com/models/gpt-5-4 comparison table already cited elsewhere in this file (page attributes 'Benchmark data from Epoch AI (CC BY) and Artificial Analysis'); fits the corpus's existing GPT-5.x AIME 2025 trajectory (94.6/100/94/100 for GPT-5/GPT-5-Pro/GPT-5.1/GPT-5.2). LiveCodeBench and MMMU: added 84.1% and 87.5% respectively, sourced to Vals.ai's own independent GPT-5.4 evaluation (https://www.vals.ai/models/openai_gpt-5.4-2026-03-05, the '(xhigh)' config, same March-5-2026 release already tracked here per this file's established treatment of xhigh data as belonging to this flagship entry). Vals.ai renders its numeric scores client-side, so the raw fetched HTML shows a '0.0%' placeholder; the true values were read from the CSS --target-width custom property on each benchmark's result bar, a technique validated by cross-checking the same page's GPQA Diamond bar (91.666%) against this file's already-confirmed, independently-sourced 92.8%/93.3% GPQA figures (within ~1-1.5pts, consistent with normal cross-harness noise already seen elsewhere in this file, e.g. the 76.9 vs 78.2 SWE-bench spread between Epoch and Vals.ai). MMLU, MATH-500, and HumanEval: checked OpenAI's own launch coverage (the-decoder.com's launch-day table already used throughout this file, and Artificial Analysis's GPT-5.4 model page), Epoch AI/Artificial Analysis's aggregated 17-benchmark GPT-5.4 table via themodelbeat.com, and Vals.ai's full 19-benchmark GPT-5.4 (xhigh) table (which tracks MMLU-Pro but not base MMLU) — none of the three publish MMLU, MATH-500, or HumanEval for GPT-5.4. Cross-referenced sibling models GPT-5, GPT-5-Pro, GPT-5.1, and GPT-5.2 already in this corpus: none of them carry MMLU, MATH, or HumanEval scores either, confirming this is a consistent multi-source, multi-generation absence (these three are treated as saturated/retired benchmarks across the whole late-GPT-5.x line) rather than a research gap, so all three are omitted per corpus convention. Comparability re-check on the existing swe-bench=76.9 entry: re-fetched themodelbeat.com/models/gpt-5-4 directly, which explicitly labels this row 'SWE-bench Verified: 76.9%', distinct from the SWE-bench Pro figure already tracked separately under swe-bench-pro=57.7; the entry's own notes text already states this correctly and only mentions 'SWE-Bench Pro' by name to draw the contrast. The flagged concern appears to be a substring-match false positive on that clarifying phrase, not an actual mislabeling -- confirmed correct, no change made.
